resources.name: Virginia Works (Virginia's Workforce Development Agency)
resources.description: Statewide workforce development agency (formerly the Virginia Employment Commission/VEC). Operates Virginia Career Works centers, administers unemployment services, the Work Opportunity Tax Credit, registered apprenticeship, and a dedicated 'Hire Returning Citizens' employer track for second-chance/fair-chance hiring.

resources.name: Virginia Career Works
resources.description: Network of regional one-stop career centers across Virginia offering free job search, resume help, computer/technology access, training referrals, and dedicated re-entry services for justice-involved jobseekers. Regional sites include Capital Region (Richmond) and the Hampton Roads area.

resources.name: Virginia Bonding Program (Federal Bonding)
resources.description: Virginia's implementation of the Federal Bonding Program, administered through the Virginia Department of Corrections. Provides a fidelity bond (up to $15,000) that insures an employer against loss for the first six months of employment when hiring an at-risk worker with a conviction, at no cost to the jobseeker or employer.

resources.name: Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC)
resources.description: Federal tax credit (administered in Virginia by Virginia Works) for employers who hire individuals from target groups, including ex-felons. Credit ranges from a minimum of $1,200 up to $9,600 per qualified hire. Employers apply through the Virginia Works WOTC online system.

resources.name: VADOC Reentry Resources
resources.description: Virginia Department of Corrections reentry portal for incarcerated individuals and those under community supervision, covering employment readiness, the bonding program, and transition support back into the community.

resources.name: Virginia Has Jobs - Fair Chance
resources.description: Career portal launched by VADOC and Virginia Works (with VCHEP and Resilience Education) to help justice-impacted Virginians explore careers, connect with fair-chance employers, and find training, registered apprenticeships, and supportive services through a career-mapping tool.

resources.name: Virginia Correctional Enterprises (VCE) Re-Entry Program
resources.description: VCE provides incarcerated individuals real-world experience in manufacturing, logistics, design and other trades inside its shops, building transferable skills and credentials that translate directly into post-release employment.

resources.name: Goodwill of Central & Coastal Virginia - Re-Entry
resources.description: Free re-entry services to reduce recidivism by helping returning citizens overcome barriers to employment, delivered at Goodwill community employment centers across 39 cities/counties in Hampton Roads and Central Virginia. Partners with the Federal Bonding Program, community colleges, DMV and other agencies.

resources.name: Jails to Jobs - Second Chance Employers Network
resources.description: National nonprofit resource connecting formerly incarcerated jobseekers with employers committed to second-chance hiring, with guidance on how to launch a second-chance hiring program and a directory of participating employers.

resources.name: Virginia Values Veterans (V3) Program
resources.description: Free Virginia Department of Veterans Services program that trains and certifies employers in best practices for recruiting, hiring and retaining veterans, and helps veterans use GI Bill benefits in on-the-job training and registered apprenticeships. Relevant for justice-involved participants who are also veterans.

resources.name: Registered Apprenticeship (Virginia Works)
resources.description: State-supported earn-while-you-learn registered apprenticeships combining paid on-the-job training with classroom instruction at community colleges and technical centers. The model is explicitly open to returning citizens, career-switchers, veterans and youth across the skilled trades.

resources.name: Honest Jobs - Fair-Chance Employment
resources.description: Online job board specifically for fair-chance employment, connecting people with criminal records to employers who actively hire returning citizens nationwide, including in Virginia.

sectors.name: Warehouse & Logistics / Distribution
sectors.outlook: Very high demand. Virginia is a major East Coast distribution hub (Port of Virginia, I-95/I-85/I-64 corridors); the Tri-Cities and Hampton Roads have dense clusters of fulfillment and distribution centers actively hiring, many with second-chance practices.

sectors.name: Transportation / CDL Trucking
sectors.outlook: Strong, persistent driver shortage statewide. Regional and OTR carriers run terminals in Richmond and Hampton; Class A CDL drivers are in high demand and command among the best wages available to program graduates.

sectors.name: Construction & Skilled Trades
sectors.outlook: High demand across Hampton Roads, Richmond Metro and the Tri-Cities for electricians, plumbers, carpenters and laborers. Registered apprenticeships provide a clear earn-while-you-learn pathway; commercial/prevailing-wage work pays well.

sectors.name: Manufacturing
sectors.outlook: Steady demand in Virginia's Gateway Region (Petersburg/Hopewell/Chesterfield) with food producers, advanced materials and national distributors hiring production and material-handling roles; many are open to second-chance candidates.

sectors.name: Food Service
sectors.outlook: Consistent openings and fast entry with low barriers, making it a common first post-release job. Wages have risen with Virginia's minimum wage; tipped front-of-house roles can earn more with tips.

sectors.name: Customer Service / Retail
sectors.outlook: Widely available across all three partner regions with relatively low barriers to entry; large retailers and call centers frequently participate in ban-the-box and second-chance hiring.

sectors.name: General Labor
sectors.outlook: Abundant openings through staffing agencies and direct hire, often a flexible bridge job. Staffing firms in Central Virginia specialize in placing workers into warehouse, manufacturing and construction support roles.

jobs.title: Electrical Apprentice
requirements: Willing to enroll in registered apprenticeship
jobs.description: Registered apprenticeship combining paid on-the-job training with classroom instruction in commercial and residential wiring. Earn while you learn toward journeyman certification. Fair-chance employer; bonding available.

jobs.title: Electrician Helper
requirements: No license required for helper role
jobs.description: Assist licensed electricians with installations, conduit, and pulling wire on commercial job sites across the Peninsula. On-the-job training provided with a path to apprenticeship. Open to returning citizens.

jobs.title: Maintenance Electrician (Facilities)
jobs.description: Perform electrical maintenance and repairs across a commercial campus, including troubleshooting motors, panels, and lighting. Steady full-time schedule with benefits in the Richmond metro.

jobs.title: Plumbing Apprentice
jobs.description: Learn the plumbing trade through a registered apprenticeship installing and repairing piping in residential and light commercial settings across the Tri-Cities. Paid training with advancement to journeyman. Second-chance friendly.

jobs.title: Plumber Helper
requirements: Physically able to dig and lift
jobs.description: Support licensed plumbers on service calls and new construction; carry materials, dig, and learn the trade hands-on. Growth path into apprenticeship. Bonding and WOTC available for eligible hires.

jobs.title: Pipefitter
jobs.description: Install and maintain process piping systems at an industrial facility in Hopewell. Strong pay reflecting Virginia prevailing wages for the trade. Steady industrial work with overtime opportunities.

jobs.title: Carpenter
requirements: Able to read a tape measure
jobs.description: Frame, hang, and finish residential and commercial projects across Hampton Roads. Experienced carpenters earn top of range; helpers welcome to start lower and grow. Fair-chance employer participating in the Virginia Bonding Program.

jobs.title: Carpenter Helper / Rough Framer
jobs.description: Assist framing crews on residential builds in the Emporia/Greensville area. On-the-job training, no prior experience required. Reentry-friendly local contractor near the Jarratt facility.

jobs.title: Construction Laborer
requirements: Able to work outdoors in all weather
jobs.description: General site labor on commercial and roadwork projects across the Tri-Cities: material handling, demolition, cleanup, and assisting trades. Entry-level with overtime. Second-chance employer; WOTC eligible.

jobs.title: Concrete Finisher
jobs.description: Pour, screed, and finish concrete for commercial flatwork and foundations in the Richmond metro. Skilled finishers earn strong wages with steady year-round work.

jobs.title: Warehouse Associate
requirements: Able to stand for full shift
jobs.description: Pick, pack, stow and ship customer orders at a high-volume fulfillment center in the Tri-Cities distribution hub. Multiple shifts available with benefits from day one. Known for hiring people with records depending on offense.

jobs.title: Material Handler
jobs.description: Move, load, and stage materials in a Hopewell distribution/manufacturing facility. Forklift certification available on the job. Reentry-friendly with WOTC and bonding support.

jobs.title: Distribution Center Associate
jobs.description: Receive, stock, and ship auto parts at a regional distribution center serving the Richmond metro. Stable full-time role with growth into lead positions. Participates in ban-the-box hiring.

jobs.title: Warehouse Associate
jobs.description: Support Port of Virginia import/export operations with loading, unloading, and inventory near the Newport News terminals. Entry-level with overtime. Fair-chance employer in the Hampton Roads logistics corridor.

jobs.title: Forklift Operator
requirements: Forklift certification or willing to certify
jobs.description: Operate sit-down and stand-up forklifts to move product in a Chesterfield distribution warehouse. Steady day shift with benefits. Certification provided for strong candidates.

jobs.title: CDL Class A Driver (Regional)
jobs.description: Regional and OTR routes out of the Richmond terminal; top drivers average around $1,500/week. Mileage-based pay with home time. A top-earning track for graduates who hold a Class A CDL.

jobs.title: CDL Class A Driver (Port/Drayage)
requirements: TWIC card or willing to obtain
jobs.description: Haul containers between the Port of Virginia terminals and regional distribution centers. Home daily, strong hourly pay. Fair-chance carrier reviewing records individually.

jobs.title: CDL Class B Delivery Driver
jobs.description: Deliver food products to retail and food-service accounts on regional routes from the Petersburg facility. Home daily with benefits. A solid step up for graduates earning their Class B.

jobs.title: Line Cook
requirements: Able to stand for full shift
Food handler card or willing to obtain
jobs.description: Prepare and plate menu items on a busy line in a Hampton Roads restaurant. Fast entry, training provided, with growth to lead cook. Reentry-friendly employer with flexible scheduling.

jobs.title: Dishwasher / Prep Cook
jobs.description: Wash dishes, basic food prep, and kitchen support for a Richmond catering operation. Low-barrier entry job ideal as a first post-release role with a path to prep and line cook. Second-chance friendly.

jobs.title: Food Service Worker
requirements: Able to stand for full shift
jobs.description: Cook, serve, and support front and back of house at a local Emporia diner near the Jarratt/Greensville facility. Training provided; tips supplement wages for some shifts. Welcoming to returning citizens.

jobs.title: Customer Service Representative
jobs.description: Handle inbound customer calls and resolve account questions for a Richmond contact center. Paid training, set schedules, and advancement to senior rep. Participates in fair-chance hiring.

jobs.title: Retail Sales Associate
jobs.description: Assist customers, stock shelves, and run registers at a Hampton retail store. Trade knowledge a plus for the contractor desk. Major retailer known for giving second chances under ban-the-box.

jobs.title: Production Associate (Manufacturing)
jobs.description: Operate and tend production lines at a food/advanced-materials manufacturer in Virginia's Gateway Region. Shift differentials and benefits; growth into machine operator and lead roles. Second-chance employer.

jobs.title: General Laborer (Temp-to-Hire)
jobs.description: Temp-to-hire general labor assignments in warehouse, manufacturing, and construction support across Central Virginia through a local staffing firm. Fast placement and a bridge to permanent roles. Works with second-chance candidates.

jobs.title: Groundskeeper / Maintenance Helper
jobs.description: Maintain grounds and assist with light maintenance at residential properties across the Peninsula: landscaping, trash, basic repairs. Entry-level with steady hours. Reentry-friendly employer.

matching_design: The app should compute a 0-100 fit score by comparing each participant's profile to a job's matched_track and requirements. Pull three participant signals: (1) completed_tracks (the trade course tracks they finished, e.g. trades-electrical), (2) readiness_milestones (resume done, mock interview passed, ID/license/CDL obtained, drug-screen ready, reliable transportation, bonding/WOTC eligibility), and (3) tier (overall program readiness level). Weight a direct matched_track equality at roughly 50%, milestone coverage of the job's requirements at roughly 35%, and tier alignment at roughly 15%, with a bonus when reentry_friendly is true. Label scores >=75 'ready to apply', 60-74 'almost ready' (surface the 1-2 missing milestones), and <60 'keep building'. Concrete fields to add: participants need completed_tracks[], milestones{}, tier, has_cdl/cdl_class, has_drivers_license, transportation_ok, bonding_eligible; jobs already carry matched_track, requirements[], reentry_friendly, region, and wage so a region/commute filter and wage floor can refine ranking.
